Overview of the Malko Competition
The Malko Competition is an international competition for young conductors, established in 1965 to honor Nicolai Malko, the founding conductor of the Danish Radio Symphony Orchestra. The competition is held every three years and attracts talented conductors aged 20 to 35 from around the world. Winners receive significant prizes, including cash awards and opportunities to conduct with major orchestras.
